title: Tasty Flaky Uruguayan Red Bean Empanadas
description: A traditional Uruguayan dish featuring anko red bean paste, these empanadas are perfect for meal prep and are jain friendly. The combination of flaky crust and jammy filling is a delight to the taste buds.

ingredients.name: egg replacement
ingredients.notes: such as flax or chia seeds mixed with water

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, salt, and sugar.
Add vegetable oil and water to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon of anko red bean paste in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough in half and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops with egg replacement and bake for 20 minutes or until golden brown.
